item.title: CVPR
item.description:
Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition

2024

2023

2022

2021

D-NeRF: Neural Radiance Fields for Dynamic Scenes

[ homepage | paper | code ]

Keywords: NeRF, dynamic scene

Aim: Extend neural radiance fields to a dynamic domain, allowing to reconstruct and render novel images of objects under rigid and non-rigid motions from a single camera moving around the scene.

Contribution:

  1. Proposed a new method: D-NeRF, which is the first approach able to generate a neural implicit representation for non-rigid and time-varying scenes, trained solely on monocular data without the need of 3D ground-truth supervision nor a multi-view camera setting.
  2. Sufficient experiments were done to demonstrate the effectiveness of proposed approach on scenes with objects under rigid, articulated and non-rigid motions.

Motivation: Considering time as an additional input to the system, so we could split the learning process in two main stages: one that encodes the scene into a canon- ical space and another that maps this canonical representation into the deformed scene at a particular time.

Architecture:

D-NeRF consists of two main neural network modules, Canonical Network and Deformation Network, which parameterize the mappings $Ψ_t$(from point’s position in time-varying scene to point’s position in canonical scene), $Ψ_x$(from point’s position & viewing direction to emitted color & volume density).

Comparison models: NeRF@ECCV'20, T-NeRF(Time-conditioned NeRF)@This paper

item.title: Others
item.description:
Other Conference/Journal

ACM MM

Proceedings of the ACM International Conference on Multimedia

2023

MICCAI

International Conference in Medical Image Computing and Computer-Assisted Intervention

2023

Frontiers of Information Technology & Electronic Engineering

2022

Visual recognition of cardiac pathology based on 3D parametric model reconstruction

[ homepage | paper ]

Keywords: Cardiac, 3D parametric model, Cardiac pathology diagnosis

Aim: Construct and use 3D parametric model as an augmentation to generate heart data for better training a classifier of cardiac pathology.

Contribution:

  1. Construct 3D cardiac parametric model for each pathology and apply cardiac visual knowledge of different cardiac pathologies as parameters to generate reasonable 3D cardiac model.
  2. Sample 3D cardiac data with changing parameters of 3D cardiac parametric model as an augmentation to avoid class imbalance.
  3. Exract cardiac disease-based features and use it to make prediction.

Motivation: Almost all the existing method use 2D slices of heart to extract features and make prediction. However, these 2D slices are collected from 3D imaging data, so using 2D slices may largely ignore geometric information characterizing adjacency in the 3D neighbourhood. Besides, after constructing 3D cardiac parametric model, generating reasonable cardiac data by changing the parameters of 3D cardiac model is a good way as data augmentation.

Architecture:

First, they reconstruct 3D model from labeled 2D images, based on which they employ Statistical Shape Model(SSM) to obtain 3D cardiac parametric model. Then, after using PCA to determine the bases of the category, they use parameter variation to make prediction. Besides above, they could also random sample the parameters to generate reasonable cardiac data as an augmentation to train a better model for cardiac pathology’s classification.

Comparison models: Cetin et al.@STACOM'17, Isensee et al.@STACOM'17, Khened et al.@STACOM'17, Wolterink@STACOM‘17, Zheng et al.@MedIA'19, Chang and Jun@NeuroComputing'20, Ammar et al.@Comput Med Imag Graph'21, Thermos@MICCAI'21
